content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA containing NNN integers, and a positive integer MMM. Find the maximum value of
Ai+Aj+((Ai‚àíAj)‚Äämod‚ÄäM)A_i + A_j + ((A_i - A_j) \bmod M)Ai‚Äã+Aj‚Äã+((Ai‚Äã‚àíAj‚Äã)modM)

across all pairs 1‚â§i,j‚â§N1 \leq i, j \leq N1‚â§i,j‚â§N.

Note that x‚Äämod‚ÄäMx \bmod MxmodM refers to the smallest **non-negative** integer obtained as the remainder upon dividing xxx by MMM. For example, 4‚Äämod‚Ää3=14 \bmod 3 = 14mod3=1 and (‚àí10)‚Äämod‚Ää3=2(-10) \bmod 3 = 2(‚àí10)mod3=2.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, the number of test cases. The description of test cases follows.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.


Output Format

For each test case, output on a new line the maximum value of Ai+Aj+((Ai‚àíAj)‚Äämod‚ÄäM)A_i + A_j + ((A_i - A_j) \bmod M)Ai‚Äã+Aj‚Äã+((Ai‚Äã‚àíAj‚Äã)modM).
constraints: The sum of NNN across all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.
The sum of NNN across all test cases won't exceed 100010001000

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There are 444 possible pairs of indices to choose from. Their respective values are:

i=1,j=1i = 1, j = 1i=1,j=1, giving 12+12+((12‚àí12)‚Äämod‚Ää18)=24+0=2412 + 12 + ((12 - 12) \bmod 18) = 24 + 0 = 2412+12+((12‚àí12)mod18)=24+0=24
i=1,j=2i = 1, j = 2i=1,j=2, giving 12+1+((12‚àí1)‚Äämod‚Ää18)=13+11=2412 + 1 + ((12 - 1) \bmod 18) = 13 + 11 = 2412+1+((12‚àí1)mod18)=13+11=24
i=2,j=1i = 2, j = 1i=2,j=1, giving 1+12+((1‚àí12)‚Äämod‚Ää18)=13+7=201 + 12 + ((1 - 12) \bmod 18) = 13 + 7 = 201+12+((1‚àí12)mod18)=13+7=20
i=2,j=2i = 2, j = 2i=2,j=2, giving 1+1+((1‚àí1)‚Äämod‚Ää18)=2+0=21 + 1 + ((1 - 1) \bmod 18) = 2 + 0 = 21+1+((1‚àí1)mod18)=2+0=2

Of these, the largest value is 242424.
Test case 222: There are 3√ó3=93\times 3 = 93√ó3=9 choices for pairs (i,j)(i, j)(i,j). Of these, one way to achieve the maximum is by choosing i=2,j=3i = 2, j = 3i=2,j=3, giving 5+6+((5‚àí6)‚Äämod‚Ää5)=11+4=155 + 6 + ((5 - 6) \bmod 5) = 11 + 4 = 155+6+((5‚àí6)mod5)=11+4=15.
Test case 333: Picking i=1,j=3i = 1, j = 3i=1,j=3 gives a value of 79+80+((79‚àí80)‚Äämod‚Ää4)=159+3=16279 + 80 + ((79 - 80) \bmod 4) = 159 + 3 = 16279+80+((79‚àí80)mod4)=159+3=162, which is the largest possible.
Test case 444: Picking i=3,j=2i = 3, j = 2i=3,j=2 gives a value of 56+46+((56‚àí46)‚Äämod‚Ää20)=102+10=11256 + 46 + ((56 - 46) \bmod 20) = 102 + 10 = 11256+46+((56‚àí46)mod20)=102+10=112, which is the largest possible.
